tex:
(a) Sam buys 3 apples at 40 cents each.
\newline

He also buys 5 peaches.
\newline

The total cost is \$3.45.
\newline

Find the cost of 1 peach.
\newline

(b) Pears cost 55 cents each.
\newline

Bananas cost 36 cents each.
\newline

Write an expression, in cents, for the cost of $x$ pears and $y$ bananas.
\newline

content.content:
Sam buys 3 apples at 40 cents each.
\newline

He also buys 5 peaches.
\newline

The total cost is \$3.45.
\newline

Pears cost 55 cents each.
\newline

Bananas cost 36 cents each.
\newline

Write an expression, in cents, for the cost of $x$ pears and $y$ bananas.
\newline

_rawBody:
\begin{parts}
\item Sam buys 3 apples at 40 cents each.

He also buys 5 peaches.

The total cost is \$3.45.

\vgap

Find the cost of 1 peach.

\vgap

\AnswerLine[][cents]
\Marks{3}

\item Pears cost 55 cents each.

Bananas cost 36 cents each.

Write an expression, in cents, for the cost of $x$ pears and $y$ bananas.

\vgap

\AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{2}
\end{parts}

qtype: laws of indices, expanding and factorising quadratic expressions
tex:
(a) $5^5 \times 5^x = 5^{20}$.
\newline

Find the value of $x$.
\newline

(b) Expand and simplify.
\newline

$(x + 5)(x - 4)$

(c) Factorise.
\newline

$r^2 - 21r - 73$
_rawBody:
\begin{parts}
\item $5^5 \times 5^x = 5^{20}$.

\vgap

Find the value of $x$.

\vgap

\AnswerLine[$x = $][]
\Marks{1}

\item Expand and simplify.

\vgap

$(x + 5)(x - 4)$

\vgap

\AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{2}

\item Factorise.

\vgap

$r^2 - 21r - 73$

\vgap

\AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{2}
\end{parts}
